prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `e2092e0c`.
codex_response:
The rule is: take the exact top-left `3x3` pattern, find its only other occurrence in the grid, and paint a `5x5` border of color `5` around that second copy. I used the `opus46` hint because it matched the official pairs; I discarded the `sonnet45` hint because it misread the task as a vague rectangle-placement problem rather than exact repeated-`3x3` matching.

The task package is in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_e2092e0c/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_e2092e0c/verifier.py#L4), [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_e2092e0c/generator.py#L6), and [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_e2092e0c/notes.md). No new primitive was needed. The generator explicitly builds the corner reference with its clipped border, places one interior copy, adds sparse random noise, and keeps the target border cells non-`5` so the output consistently paints the full frame.

Validation passed end to end: official verifier `4/4`, generated verifier `1000/1000`. Artifacts are at [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/e2092e0c/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/e2092e0c/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/e2092e0c/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/e2092e0c/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/e2092e0c/previews/preview_03.png). I couldn’t use the local image viewer in this interface, but I did sanity-check the built set structurally: all 1000 examples are `15x15`, each has exactly two occurrences of the reference `3x3`, and nonzero counts stay in a plausible range (`75`-`98`, mean `86.9`).
